$comment: Read-back differential for the PUBLIC wardrobe write path (seam #7): v4's
WardrobeRepository.create/update/delete, ported to
quilltap-core::db::vault_wardrobe_public. One committed file drives BOTH sides:
- the v4 oracle (build-vault-wardrobe-public-fixture.ts bakes a character +
empty vault via the REAL repos.characters.create; vault-wardrobe-public.ts
drives the REAL repos.wardrobe public CRUD + reads back).
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness vault_wardrobe_public test) composes the
verified leaves (read_character_vault_wardrobe + project_vault_wardrobe +
detect_component_cycles + characters find_by_id_raw) and reads back.
WHY READ-BACK, NOT A TIER-2 TABLE DUMP: build_wardrobe_item_file writes the
item's minted `updatedAt` into the content-addressed .md, so an update's fresh
timestamp is buried inside a content SHA a byte-level dump can't normalize. The
read parses updatedAt back out as a structured field, where it normalizes
cleanly. Both sides run the SAME op sequence against a copy of the same baked
fixture, then read back each character's Wardrobe/ via readCharacterVaultWardrobe
(itself already verified equivalent). The projection PRIMITIVE is separately
byte-verified (vault_wardrobe_write_equivalence).
SCOPE: only the CHARACTER tier. create pins id/createdAt/updatedAt via
CreateOptions so the create bytes are deterministic; update mints updatedAt
(normalized on both sides). The General/project archetype tiers + per-mount
write serialization are out of scope (same deferral as read_character_vault_wardrobe).
OPS bank: create (op1), a composite create referencing op1 by id (op2, its
componentItems slug round-trips), a rename update (op3, old file swept + composite
slug recomputed), a cycle-forming update that THROWS (op4, folder unchanged), a
real delete (op5, and the surviving composite's now-dangling ref DROPS on read),
a delete of the already-gone id returning false (op6), and a create against a
non-existent character that THROWS no-mount (op7, folder unchanged).
NORMALIZATION (harness, both sides): each read-back item's `updatedAt` -> <ts>;
everything else exact (createdAt stays pinned, proving preservation on update).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one folders/tags/
characters use). NOT the real Friday pepper; safe to commit.

character.name: Wardrobe Owner
character.identity: The keeper of a modest closet.
